title: The AI-Augmented Advisor: How AI Will Reshape Wealth Management by 2030
executive_summary: Deloitte projects that by 2030, 60% of wealth management client touchpoints will be AI-driven — up from under 10% today — with human advisors increasingly focused on complex planning, emotional support, and trust-building
The advisor-to-client ratio will shift dramatically: AI will allow the best advisors to manage 3-5x their current book size without service degradation
The report introduces the concept of the 'hybrid advisor' — a human advisor whose judgment is continuously augmented by real-time AI insights — as the dominant model by 2028

Three revenue pools are identified as at-risk: portfolio construction fees (commoditized by AI), plan preparation (automated), and routine client communication (replaced by AI). Trust advisory and complex planning fees are seen as durable
Deloitte recommends wealth management firms invest in AI training for advisors now — the gap between AI-fluent and AI-naive advisors will translate directly into client outcomes by 2027
the_one_insight: The hybrid advisor model — human judgment continuously informed by AI — will be the dominant competitive format within five years. The window to build this capability is closing.

key_quotes.text: The advisors who will thrive in the next decade are not those who resist AI, nor those who are replaced by it. They are those who learn to direct it — to ask it the right questions and trust the right answers.
key_quotes.context: On the evolving advisor skill set

key_quotes.text: We are not heading toward a world with fewer advisors. We are heading toward a world where fewer advisors serve dramatically more clients, at higher quality, for lower cost. That is a reckoning for the industry's current operating model.
key_quotes.context: On the structural change ahead for wealth management
